items.headline: AI Concerns and Rising Oil Prices Weigh on Wall Street, Halting Record Rally
items.summary: U.S. equities closed mixed on Tuesday, with a cautious sentiment dominating as investors digested concerns over AI spending and surging crude oil prices. A report on OpenAI's missed revenue targets triggered a sell-off in technology stocks, while ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated. The Dow managed modest gains, but the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite pulled back from recent record highs. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Coca-Cola ($KO) Rallies on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Coca-Cola shares climbed after the beverage giant reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter profit and revenue, fueled by strong performance in China, the United States, and India.

items.headline: Sanmina ($SANM) Soars on Earnings Beat
items.summary: Electronics contract manufacturer Sanmina saw its shares rise after reporting better-than-expected second-quarter results and providing a positive full-year outlook.

items.headline: Nvidia ($NVDA) Leads Tech Decline Amid AI Doubts
items.summary: Nvidia, a key player in AI chips, was a significant drag on the S&P 500 as concerns about OpenAI's revenue targets and the sustainability of AI spending rattled the technology sector.

items.headline: Spotify ($SPOT) Falls on Underwhelming Outlook
items.summary: Spotify Technology shares dropped significantly after the company's first-quarter results underwhelmed Wall Street, with its operating income forecast for the current quarter missing analysts' estimates.

items.headline: Rambus ($RMBS) Plunges on Mixed Q1 Results
items.summary: Rambus shares plunged after its first-quarter results, while largely in line with expectations, were seen as a disappointment by analysts following recent strength in the stock, leading to a downgrade.

items.headline: OpenAI Revenue Report Fuels Tech Sector Sell-off
items.summary: A Wall Street Journal report indicating that OpenAI missed internal targets for weekly users and revenue sparked fears of overspending on artificial intelligence infrastructure, causing a broad decline in semiconductor, software, and hardware stocks.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Surge Amid Iran War and Supply Concerns
items.summary: Brent crude oil prices climbed above $110 a barrel, and WTI crude traded near $100 a barrel, driven by escalating tensions in the US-Iran conflict and continued disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, raising inflation concerns.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Begins Key Monetary Policy Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) commenced its two-day meeting to discuss monetary policy. No change in interest rates is expected, but investors will be keen on policymakers' assessment of inflation and the economy. This is anticipated to be the final meeting chaired by Jerome Powell.

items.headline: Technology Lags While Energy and Defensive Sectors Show Resilience
items.summary: The Information Technology sector was the worst-performing major sector today, contrasting with Monday's performance, as doubts about AI growth led to declines. Meanwhile, the Energy sector demonstrated strength due to rising oil prices, and defensive stocks supported the Dow.

items.headline: UPS Reports Q1 Earnings, Reaffirms Full-Year Guidance
items.summary: UPS announced first-quarter 2026 consolidated revenues of $21.2 billion, with diluted earnings per share of $1.02. Despite beating profit expectations, the company reaffirmed its full-year guidance, leading to a dip in stock price.
